$comment: Recipe consumed by ~/.claude/skills/bump/SKILL.md. Vestige's project() block spans multiple lines, so version_pattern targets the indented VERSION line.
$todos_run_before_post_check: Todo 1 below MUST be done BEFORE running `post_check`. The post_check is the version-edit hook which compares CMakeLists.txt VERSION against CHANGELOG.md's topmost `## [X.Y.Z]` heading; if CHANGELOG still says `[Unreleased]`, the post_check passes (Unreleased is treated as in-sync), but the release is incomplete. Run todo 1, then post_check, then audit + tag.
todos: Promote `## [Unreleased]` in CHANGELOG.md to `## [{NEW}] - {TODAY}` and write the body. Or, if [Unreleased] is empty, add a fresh `## [{NEW}] - {TODAY}` section above [Unreleased]. **Required before post_check meaningfully validates.** Consider invoking the `changelog-writer` subagent to draft the body from the commit range since the previous tag.
Run post_check: `bash tools/hook-on-version-edit.sh < <(echo '{"tool_name":"Edit","tool_input":{"file_path":"CMakeLists.txt"}}')`. Confirm no systemMessage emitted (= no drift).
Run the audit: `python3 tools/audit/audit.py -t 1 2 3` and pipe findings to the `audit-triage` subagent before committing — typical filter ratio is ~85% noise.
Tag the commit: `git tag v{NEW}` (Vestige convention from prior releases).
